summary: Decompiled the Marumine/Electrode item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itmarumine.s with src/melee/it/items/itmarumine.c, wiring the C object into the build, and updating symbols/header declarations. The new C recreates the item state table it_803F7E78, item callbacks from it_802D09D0 through it_802D1410, hitlag effect pause/resume setup, animation state changes, fall/ground transitions, and repeated efSync_Spawn accessory effects. Most internal callbacks and constants were marked local/static in symbols.txt; the PR author specifically noted adding scope:local for fn_802D0F98 because that symbol previously lacked an explicit scope.
what_changed: Deleted the 851-line assembly implementation asm/melee/it/items/itmarumine.s and added a 399-line C implementation in src/melee/it/items/itmarumine.c.
Changed the build from assembling melee/it/items/itmarumine.s to compiling melee/it/items/itmarumine.c in both configure.py and obj_files.mk.
Added it_802D09D0 to src/melee/it/items/itmarumine.h and removed the old declaration from src/melee/it/items/it_27CF.h, consolidating the symbol under the item-specific header.
Included itmarumine.h from src/melee/it/it_26B1.c, indicating call sites now use the item-specific header.
Added prototypes for helper APIs needed by the decompilation: it_8026E71C in it_266F.h and it_80274250 in it_2725.h.
Extended the pokemon item variable struct in itCommonItems.h with an int x64 field, used by the new C as ip->xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.x64 for an effect/timer counter.
Recreated static Vec3 const it_803B86C8 = { 0 } and the ItemStateTable it_803F7E78 with seven state rows mapping to callbacks such as it_802D1384, it_802D0B74, it_802D0CEC, it_802D12F4, and others.

Implemented item behavior around Item_80268E5C state changes, efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag hooks, lb_8000BA0C animation frame-speed updates, efSync_Spawn(0x471, ...), it_80272860 fall physics, and transition helpers including it_8026E15C, it_8026D62C, it_8026E71C, and it_8027A118.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so many itmarumine-internal callbacks and constants changed from scope:global to scope:local, including it_802D0AAC, it_802D0B04, it_802D0DBC, it_802D1204, it_803B86C8, it_804DD440, it_804DD444, and it_804DD448.
Added explicit scope:local to fn_802D0F98 in symbols.txt; the PR body calls out that this was unusual because the symbol previously had no scope annotation.
smart_moves: Kept the exported surface small by making internal callbacks static in C and matching that with scope:local symbol entries.
Preserved address comments on static prototypes, making it easy to map each C function back to the removed assembly addresses during review and future matching work.
Used existing item macros/types such as GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, Item_GObj, ItemStateTable, ItemAttr, and itPokemonAttributes instead of raw object offsets where known.
Introduced the pokemon x64 field in a shared struct rather than repeatedly using an opaque raw offset for the accessory-effect counter.
Handled the previously nameless/local accessory callback fn_802D0F98 explicitly as a static function and documented its local symbol scope in symbols.txt.
Moved it_802D09D0 declaration out of a generic/incorrect item header and into itmarumine.h, reducing cross-item header leakage.
decomp_lessons: When an asm item module is converted to C, update both build systems in lockstep: configure.py and obj_files.mk.
For item state tables, declare static callback prototypes before the table so function-pointer arrays can be represented directly in C.
If functions are only referenced inside the same item module or through local tables/callback fields, mark them static and convert symbols.txt scope from global to local.
Accessory callbacks assigned through ip->on_accessory may need explicit local symbol entries even when their generated name is fn_######## rather than it_########.
Shared item variable structs should be extended when a decompiled module reveals a repeated semantic slot, as with pokemon.x64 used alongside pokemon.timer.
A zero vector constant from rodata can often be represented as static Vec3 const name = { 0 }, with symbols.txt scope adjusted to local if module-private.

Preserve behavior-affecting side effects around item state changes: hitlag pause/resume callbacks, animation update flags, JObj flags, physics transitions, and effect spawning all appear necessary for matching the original module.
naming_conventions: The module keeps address-based placeholder names for unknown functions: it_802D09D0, it_802D0A0C, it_802D1204, etc.
The odd local accessory callback remains named fn_802D0F98 rather than being forced into an it_ prefix, matching the existing symbol name.
Static module data keeps generated names it_803B86C8 and it_803F7E78.
Known engine/library APIs retain their established project names and casing, including Item_80268E5C, Item_8026AE84, efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, efSync_Spawn, and lb_8000BA0C.
Unknown pokemon attribute/variable fields are still placeholder names such as attr->x0, attr->xC, attr->timer, attr->max, and pokemon.x64.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated simple assembly stubs into empty void functions or false-returning bool functions where the assembly was just blr or li r3,0; blr.
Used static function declarations to match formerly local branch targets and state-table callbacks without exporting unnecessary globals.
Matched floating constants via local sdata2 symbols: 0.0F/1.0F and the int-to-double comparison constant represented by it_804DD440, it_804DD444, and it_804DD448 in symbols.txt.
Represented the repeated effect-spawn stack temporary as f32 sp10 = 1.0F before calling efSync_Spawn(0x471, gobj, bone, &sp10).
Used PAD_STACK(8) in it_802D1204, indicating stack-shape padding was required for matching that function.
Converted the asm state table data into a C ItemStateTable initializer with callback triplets for states 0 through 6.
Matched callback field stores by assigning ip->on_accessory, ip->entered_hitlag, and ip->exited_hitlag directly in C.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the provided PR slice.
The only human note is from the PR body: the author called out adding scope:local for fn_802D0F98 in symbols.txt because they had not seen that case before and wanted it noted in case it mattered.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs where fn_######## symbols needed explicit scope:local in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Search for other pokemon item modules using xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.x64 to confirm or improve the field name.
Compare itmarumine.c with neighboring pokemon item decompilations for shared patterns around efSync_Spawn(0x471) and on_accessory callbacks.
Audit item headers for declarations that still live in generic headers but belong in item-specific headers.
Look for other asm-to-C item conversions that required PAD_STACK in destruction or transition functions.
